briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 zoning bylaw with 2026 version
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing Burnaby's old Zoning Bylaw from 1965 and replacing it with a new Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change also includes new Development Permit Area Guidelines and a reassignment of zoning districts. If approved, the new bylaw would update the rules for what you can build and where, affecting many property owners and businesses in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new rules could affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to help the Housing Authority run its programs for another year. A final decision has not been made yet.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

briefs.title: Council to vote on 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed property tax rates for 2026. This includes the municipal portion (city services), the regional portion (services shared across the Metro Vancouver region), and special rates for businesses located in a Business Improvement Area (BIA). If approved, these rates will determine how much property tax you pay next year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will discuss and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these rates will affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the authority to approve or reject plans for new subdivisions (dividing land into smaller lots) and road closures. This decision is about who will hold that official role, not about changing any rules. If approved, the appointed officer would handle these land-use decisions for the city.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now — this is an internal city staffing decision.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the zoning bylaw — in this case, the rules about how tall retaining walls can be. The owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current bylaw allows. Council will decide whether to approve this variance at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variances in your area.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on insurance contract renewals
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This is a routine renewal of insurance coverage that protects city assets and operations. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting and has not yet been approved.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. This is a request for approval to send commission members to the conference, not a final decision yet.

key_dates.label: 2026 Heritage BC Conference in Squamish takes place

briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine technology purchase that keeps city systems running, but it does not directly create new rules or fees for local businesses.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for the upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the city's external audit services. This means council will decide whether to approve a change to the agreement with the company that audits the city's financial records. The decision is not final yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the consultant's work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant program. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the grants would provide funding to local community groups and non-profits. The vote will happen at an upcoming council meeting. Business owners who partner with or rely on community organizations should watch for the outcome, as it could affect local programs and services.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Fees and Charges Bylaw
briefs.summary: Council will consider mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update certain fees the city charges for various services and permits. The changes are not yet approved — council will vote on them at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these fee changes could affect your business licence or permit costs.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for Council vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and framework. If approved, this bylaw would create new rules for landlords, including rules about when and how they can end a tenancy, renovate a unit, or increase rent. The goal is to give renters more stability and prevent 'renovictions' (when a landlord evicts a tenant to do major renovations and then rents the unit out at a much higher price). Council will consider endorsing the framework and the bylaw at an upcoming meeting. This is a proposal — it has not been passed yet.
briefs.action_required: Landlords and property managers in Burnaby should watch for the council decision and review the proposed rules to prepare for possible new requirements.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety features along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should watch for the outcome, as construction could affect traffic, parking, and customer access.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on your business access and parking.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new 'protected intersection' designs — special curb and lane layouts that separate pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles. If approved, this would apply to new developments and road projects in the Town Centre area. The goal is to make streets safer for people walking and biking, especially for all ages and abilities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new development plans in the Town Centre will need to include protected intersection designs.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption via building bylaw
briefs.summary: Council will vote on a staff recommendation not to exempt Brentwood Park properties from provincial small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) requirements. Staff report that restrictive covenants are private interests the City is not party to, and that amending the Building Bylaw to require covenant compliance would need to apply city-wide to all properties with such covenants, including commercial and industrial, creating added costs and process delays. The report recommends against this approach.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on April 28, 2026 — if you own a Brentwood Park property with a restrictive covenant, this decision affects your development options.

key_dates.label: Burnaby council vote on Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption

briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information-only item — no vote or decision is required. The report gives Council a snapshot of construction activity in the city during that month.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider an 'as-found report' and 'Statement of Significance' for the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. A Statement of Significance is a document that explains why a building is historically important. This is the first step toward possibly adding the property to the city's heritage register. If approved, future changes to the building could require special permits. The decision has not been made yet — Council is reviewing the information now.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if the property is designated, any renovations or demolition will need heritage permits.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
briefs.summary: Council will consider a summary of the City of Burnaby's solid waste and recycling collection system performance for 2025. This report is for information and discussion only — no new rules or fees are proposed at this time. The report covers how well the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection programs are working.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

briefs.title: Council to consider operational update on food sustainability measures
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an operational update on food sustainability measures. This is a report to Council, not a final decision. The update will outline current programs and potential future actions to improve food sustainability in the city. No new rules or bylaws are being voted on at this time. The purpose is to inform Council and the public about ongoing work.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Safety improvements at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal includes changes to the road and intersection design to make it safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. This is not yet approved — Council will consider the plan at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, construction could affect traffic and access near your business.
briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on rezoning for hotel, more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning amendment for a large development at 3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Road, and 9858/9898 Gatineau Place. The developer wants to change an already-approved plan. Instead of building some office space, they want to add a hotel and restaurant. They also want to add 362 more condominium (strata) units, making the two towers taller — one by seven storeys, the other by four. The developer also proposes to move some required affordable housing (called 'inclusionary requirements') from this site to another site called Carrigan. This is a proposed change, not a done deal.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use tower at 6535 Kingsway, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would allow a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. The project includes 290 residential units, ground-floor commercial space facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This is a proposal only — it has not been approved yet. The rezoning application (REZ #25-09) would change the current zoning rules to permit this development. The plan matches the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan for this area.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would allow a five-storey enclosed self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. The rezoning application (REZ #25-21) would change the current zoning rules to permit this new construction. The proposal follows the city's 1998 Official Community Plan (OCP) — the long-term plan for growth and land use — and approved transition rules. This is not yet approved; Council will decide at a future meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to voice your support or concerns about this rezoning.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how you operate if your business uses e-bikes or e-scooters for deliveries or services.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in some Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The proposed change would amend the existing Parks Regulation Bylaw (1979) to create rules for where and how these electric devices can be used. This is not yet law — council will debate and decide on the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, the pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes or e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for violations related to these devices. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how e-bike and e-scooter rules are enforced in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets new property tax rates
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to set the 2026 tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby. This bylaw, called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026, is required under sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (the main law that gives BC cities their powers). If approved, it will determine how much property tax you pay next year based on your property's assessed value. The decision has not been made yet — council is set to vote on this proposal.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on this bylaw — it will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
